instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Is an Insurance Endorsement?
Meta Description: Knowing about different aspects related to term insurance, like insurance policy endorsement, can keep you informed about your options.
Keywords: insurance policy endorsement, term insurance, what is a term insurance plan, term insurance calculator
Introduction
Any addition that is made to a current/existing insurance plan is called an insurance policy endorsement. This addition alters the scope or terms of an existing policy. Endorsements are also commonly known as ‘riders’ in the field of insurance. Moreover, they apply to various kinds of insurance plans, from term insurance to vehicle insurance. It's worth finding out more about insurance endorsements and how these work.
Definition and Examples of Endorsement
A ‘rider’ or an insurance endorsement is used to change the coverage offered by any given plan. Consequently, it functions to exclude, add, or delete any clauses concerning coverage by the insurance policy. An insurance policy endorsement can be issued at the time when you buy the plan, in the duration of the term of your policy, or at the time when you opt to renew the policy. For instance, insurance endorsements may add or delete people , or locations covered by a given insurance policy. Additionally, you should be aware of the fact that an insurance endorsement is an alteration that is legally binding in any insurance policy.
The commonest example of a rider in an insurance policy is that of add-on coverage for car insurance policies. These are typically provisions to extend the scope of any vehicle policy that broaden its scope of coverage. For instance, roadside assistance is an add-on that is frequently opted for in car insurance policies. You may also find examples of insurance policy endorsement in term insurance policies, as policies are introducing the flexibility to alter particular factors of coverage (by incorporating add on covers). For instance, riders could be made in consideration of the type of plan you choose.
How Insurance Endorsement Works
Any policy alteration that is added to an insurance policy during its term would constitute an insurance policy endorsement. Moreover, this is done during the original term of the policy without renewing it. When you choose to buy any kind of insurance plan, say term insurance, you obviously take many variables into consideration before making a choice. For instance, the insurance premium may be one of these factors. You may use a term insurance calculator to calculate your premium amount and then opt for any given plan. When a policy change or an endorsement is made in your current policy, your premium may change as well. This may be an obvious result you expect as you are adding to the scope of your existing plan, hence this will cost more.
It is important to note that an insurance policy endorsement stays in force until the conclusion of your policy. If renewal of any given policy is applicable, as different policies have different terms and conditions where renewal is concerned, then you may renew the policy with the endorsements intact. However, you should be aware that, in case the insurance policy endorsement stipulates a specific timeframe, this may not be possible upon renewal. All this, of course, depends on the unique policy that you have chosen and its conditions of operation. If you opt for an endorsement in your policy, it is important for you to check it against your original policy’s declaration section. You can then make out what has exactly changed and the consequences of this.
Types of Endorsements
You may have already got the answer to the question, “What is a term insurance plan?”, before you opt for riders or endorsements. Riders may be documents that are added to your existing policy, or they may completely replace your original policy. Here are common changes that are made to policies:
Sometimes, a rider may be a simple change you need to make to a policy that has nothing to do with coverage. For instance, if your address changes during the duration of your policy’s term, you have to inform your insurer. Your insurer then sends you an endorsement mentioning your new place of residence. The old policy stating your previous address stands invalid. The rider has replaced your original insurance document.
At other times, riders may include documents that are supplementary to your original policy. When an endorsement adds a condition, for example, you get additional documents. Additionally, you may also have an endorsement added for limitations to your policy. In such cases, policies themselves remain unchanged, but only new conditions and terms are added. The insurance policy endorsement thus becomes an add-on to your plan, whether you have a term insurance plan or any other. This document should be attached to your original policy.
What does it cover?
Endorsements offer coverage for a wide variety of needs. For example, a spouse may wish to have a rider removing an ex-spouse from a home insurance policy in the event of a divorce of a couple. The spouse who requests the endorsement may then receive a new policy reflecting the changes. Another example can be sourced from a term insurance policy. You may have the names of more than one beneficiary, and you may want to change the beneficiaries or delete a name. You will get a new policy to reflect the insurance policy endorsement. With amendments to any policy or plan, you can get coverage for a wide range of conditions. However, when you buy your plan, you should check if endorsements are permitted and what they can potentially cover.
Conclusion
Endorsements are either created as a supplementary document to a policy which you already have, or can replace your old policy, depending on your insurer and the changes to be made. Endorsements give you cover for a range of circumstances, and can include deleting those insured by policies, changing technical details like addresses, or adding more coverage conditions and clauses.
Sources:
https://www.thebalance.com/insurance-endorsement-or-rider-2645729#:~:text=An%20insurance%20endorsement%20is%20a,force%20until%20your%20policy%20ends
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/term-insurance-plans/smart-protect-goal-term-plan.html
